name: Throne room
description: Small ceremonial audience chamber: an intimate, fully-enclosed throne room with a single @stairs throne on a 1-block raised @accent dais flush against the far short wall, two short @accent columns flanking the throne, @carpet banners on the throne wall, a short carpet runner from the entrance to the dais, side benches against the long walls, hanging lanterns under a flat @slab ceiling, and a treasure chest tucked beside the throne. Distinct from a great hall by being smaller, lower, fully roofed, and stripped of pillared aisles or braziers — it reads as a private audience chamber rather than a long ceremonial hall.

required_furniture.rule: A single @stairs block centered on the dais against the far short wall, facing into the room toward the entrance.
A 1-block raised platform of @accent under the throne, 3 wide × 2 deep, flush against the far short wall.
@carpet blocks affixed to the interior face of the far short wall as banner proxies, one on either side of the throne (above the dais).
Two short @accent pillars (1×3 tall) standing one cell off the dais on either side, framing the throne and the foot of the runner.
A single-cell-wide @carpet strip running 5-6 blocks from the foot of the dais toward the entrance short wall, never under the throne.
@stairs benches against the long walls, facing into the room (one or two per wall), aligned with the runner.
minecraft:lantern[hanging=true] dangling one cell below the @slab ceiling, evenly spaced along the central axis above the runner.
A minecraft:chest on the floor beside the dais (preferably on the throne's right hand), within arm's reach of the seated monarch.

placement_rules: Throne centered on far wall, raised on dais
Banners (@carpet) only on the far short wall above the throne, never on the long walls
Side benches face inward toward the runner
Lanterns hang from the @slab ceiling along the central axis, never block the throne or runner
lighting_ventilation: Fully enclosed: a flat @slab ceiling caps the room, with 2-3 minecraft:lantern hanging fixtures dangling along the central axis as the sole light source. No windows by default — this is a private audience chamber, lit ceremonially from above rather than by daylight.

medieval.notes: Stone-brick walls and andesite dais; red carpet runner and red banner-carpets behind the throne; iron lantern fixtures for a castle audience chamber.

modern.notes: Minimalist civic audience room: quartz walls, andesite dais, gray runner, recessed lantern fittings. Banner-carpets read as accent panels rather than heraldry.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak chamber with a purpur dais and columns; purple-carpet runner and banners; sea-lantern fixtures read as enchanted lights over a sorcerer's audience seat.

python_skill_signature: pipeline.skills.throne_room.build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, **kwargs) -> list[Op]
style_affinity: Medieval / fortified-military motif. Coherent in medieval/castle/rustic/fantasy/gothic. Out of place in Modern, Minimalist, East-Asian, Classical/Renaissance buildings.
